title: Daily Brief and Digest for Real Estate
metaDescription: A done-for-you morning brief and daily digest for real estate teams. New leads, hot listings, showings, and follow-ups in one short email each day.
excerpt: A done-for-you morning brief that lands in your inbox before your first showing. New leads, listing activity, and follow-ups, all in one short read.

sections.intro:
In real estate, mornings are won or lost in the first hour. New leads came in overnight from portals, your site, and referrals. Listings moved. Buyers replied. A seller wants an update. By the time you check three inboxes, a CRM, and a portal dashboard, you have already lost the showing window.

The AIOS sends you one short brief each morning. It pulls from the tools you already run on, ranks what matters, and tells you the three to five moves that will actually move deals today. You stop digging through tabs and start the day on offense.

This is a done-for-you setup. We install it, tune it to your market and voice, and operate it. You read the brief on your phone with coffee, and the system handles the routine follow-ups in the background.
sections.fourCFit:
This playbook sits in the Cadence layer of your AIOS. Cadence is the layer that runs on a schedule so work happens without you asking. It depends on the other three layers to do its job well:

  • Cadence (this page): the brief runs every morning at the time you choose, plus an optional end of day digest.
  • Context: the system knows your active listings, your pipeline stages, your service area, and how you triage buyers and sellers.
  • Connections: it reads from your CRM, inbox, calendar, and lead sources so the brief reflects live data, not yesterday's snapshot.
  • Capabilities: it can pair with the 60-Second Lead Engine so new overnight leads are already answered before the brief lands.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
Each morning you get one email or message, readable in under two minutes, with clear sections and links back to the source record.

  • Delivery SLA: brief lands by 7:00 a.m. local time, seven days a week, with 99 percent on-time delivery.
  • New leads overnight: name, source, intent signal, and whether the 60-second reply already went out.
  • Hot pipeline: deals with movement in the last 24 hours and what is waiting on you.
  • Today's calendar: showings, calls, and inspections, with addresses and contact context inline.
  • Follow-ups due: buyers and sellers who have gone quiet past your threshold, with a one-tap draft reply.
  • Listing activity: price changes, new comps in your farm area, and saved-search matches for active buyers.
  • End of day digest (optional): what closed, what slipped, and what is queued for tomorrow.
sections.howWeInstall:
1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we look at where your leads come from, which tools hold the truth, and what you actually want to see at 7 a.m. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we connect your CRM, inbox, calendar, and lead sources, write the ranking rules in your voice, and ship the first brief to you for a five-day tuning window. You approve the format before it goes live.
  3. Operate (ongoing): we run the cadence, monitor delivery, and ship one new automation per week, whether that is a seller update digest, a weekly farm area report, or a teammate version of the brief. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Time saved: 45 to 75 minutes per agent per day, mostly from killing the morning tab-hunt and the end of day status check.
  • Faster lead handling: overnight leads are answered and on the brief by 7 a.m., so the first contact happens before competitors are awake.
  • Fewer dropped follow-ups: teams typically cut stalled-buyer leakage by 20 to 35 percent within 60 days.
  • Better Monday standups: the digest becomes the single source of truth for what is real in the pipeline.
  • Standing guarantee: across your AIOS, we recover 40 or more hours a month for you, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will the brief work if my team uses different CRMs or inboxes?

Yes. The Connections layer is tool-agnostic, so the brief reads from whatever you already use. If two agents are on different systems, each gets a brief tuned to their own data.

Can I get a team version that shows everyone's pipeline?

Yes. We can run a personal brief for each agent and a team digest for the broker or team lead, with the same delivery SLA. We set the rollup rules during install.

What if I want it at 6 a.m. or want a midday update too?

The Cadence layer is built for this. We can run the brief at any local time, add a midday or end of day digest, and pause it on days you choose, like Sundays or vacation weeks. You own the schedule.
